terms.definition: Training on unlabeled data by predicting targets derived from the data itself via pretext tasks. Canonical definition in this topic.
An auxiliary prediction problem constructed from raw data (e.g., mask tokens, predict rotation) used to learn representations before downstream use.
Synonym for pretext task — a stand-in objective whose labels come from data structure rather than human annotation.
A training target automatically generated from the input (masked token, next token, rotation class) rather than supplied by a human annotator.
Supervisory signal extracted algorithmically from unlabeled examples — the defining property of self-supervised learning in this roadmap.
Informal shorthand for the self-supervised paradigm: the model supervises itself using structure present in the training corpus.
